description: Create a CardEditorPanel side panel component and integrate it into App.jsx. The panel shows title and content editors for the currently selected card (when exactly one card is selected). It follows the same side panel pattern as PinPanel.jsx (w-80, border-l, bg-white). Changes in the panel immediately update the card on the canvas via updateNode.
steps: Step 1: Create src/CardEditorPanel.jsx - a new side panel component. Props: selectedNode (the single selected node object or null), onUpdateNode (callback to update title/content), onClose (callback to close the panel). Structure: (a) If no selectedNode, show an empty state message like 'Select a single card to edit'. (b) If selectedNode exists, show: a header with a Pencil icon, the card title text, and an X close button; an input/textarea for editing the title; a large textarea for editing the content (with generous height like min-h-[200px]); and below the textarea, a markdown preview section using the existing MarkdownRenderer component (always rendered with isZoomedIn=true for full markdown rendering). Style it exactly like PinPanel - w-80 bg-white border-l border-slate-200 flex flex-col overflow-hidden shrink-0. Use Tailwind classes consistent with the project. Import Pencil and X from lucide-react.
Step 2: In src/App.jsx, add state: const [showCardEditorPanel, setShowCardEditorPanel] = useState(false); (add near line 403 where other panel states are defined).
Step 3: In src/App.jsx, add the import for CardEditorPanel at the top imports section (near line 16 where PinPanel is imported): import CardEditorPanel from './CardEditorPanel';

Step 4: In src/App.jsx, compute the selected node for the panel. Add a derived value: const cardEditorNode = (selectedNodeIds.length === 1) ? nodes.find(n => n.id === selectedNodeIds[0]) : null; (place this near line 1013 where nodes/edges/groups are derived from activeWs).

Step 5: In src/App.jsx, render the CardEditorPanel in the JSX after the PinPanel section (around line 5580). Use the same conditional pattern: {showCardEditorPanel && viewMode === 'canvas' && (<CardEditorPanel selectedNode={cardEditorNode} onUpdateNode={(updates) => { if (cardEditorNode) { takeSnapshot(); updateNode(cardEditorNode.id, updates); } }} onClose={() => setShowCardEditorPanel(false)} />)}

Step 6: In src/App.jsx, add a toggle button in the sidebar panel buttons section (around line 4353, after the Reminders button). Pattern: <button onClick={() => setShowCardEditorPanel(!showCardEditorPanel)} title='Card Editor (E)' className={`flex items-center justify-center gap-1.5 px-3 py-1.5 rounded-lg text-xs font-bold transition-all mt-1.5 w-full ${showCardEditorPanel ? 'bg-cyan-100 text-cyan-700 border border-cyan-300' : 'bg-slate-100 text-slate-500 hover:text-slate-700 hover:bg-slate-200'}`}><Pencil className='w-3.5 h-3.5' /> Card Editor</button>

Step 7: Ensure that the CardEditorPanel component handles the case where selectedNode transitions from a node to null gracefully (user deselects), and where selectedNodeIds has multiple entries (show empty state). The panel should only show editor fields when exactly one card is selected.
acceptance_criteria: A new file src/CardEditorPanel.jsx exists with a functional React component
The panel is rendered in App.jsx and toggled via showCardEditorPanel state
When exactly one card is selected (selectedNodeIds has one entry), the panel shows title input and content textarea pre-filled with the card data
Editing title or content in the panel immediately calls updateNode and updates the card on canvas
When no card or multiple cards are selected, the panel shows an empty/informational state
A toggle button exists in the sidebar to open/close the panel
The panel includes a markdown preview below the content editor using MarkdownRenderer
npm run build completes without errors
verification: Run `npm run build` and confirm it completes with no errors
Verify src/CardEditorPanel.jsx exists and exports a default component
Grep for 'CardEditorPanel' in App.jsx to confirm it is imported and rendered
